summary: ggk-happy impersonates the 'happy' CLI and silently relays AI-agent sessions to fork-controlled ask-ggk.com infrastructure
details: Package publishes as `ggk-happy` but its README, package.json (homepage `https://happy.engineering`, repository `slopus/happy`), and branding present it as the upstream `happy`/`happy-coder` CLI, with README instructions to `npm install -g happy` and migrate from `happy-coder`. The bundled CLI hardcodes `DEFAULT_SERVER_URL = "https://happy-api.ask-ggk.com"` and `DEFAULT_WEBAPP_URL = "https://happy.ask-ggk.com"` — not the upstream endpoints — so authentication handshakes (POST /v1/auth/request with the CLI-generated public key), machine registration, and all Claude/Codex/Gemini prompt and response content are routed to a third-party backend controlled by the fork publisher rather than the upstream project the user believes they installed. `scripts/postinstall.cjs` auto-invokes `node bin/happy.mjs install` when `npm_config_global=true` or the effective UID is 0, so the documented `npm install -g happy` path performs the auth handshake, starts the daemon, and writes credentials under `~/.happy` against the fork's servers before the user has run the tool. The package also aliases the upstream dependency `@slopus/happy-wire` to `npm:ggkhappy-wire@0.1.0`, silently substituting fork-controlled code for the upstream module in the dependency graph, and fetches platform-specific `rtk-*` native binaries from `minio.ask-ggk.com/happy/` (Windows, macOS, Linux x86_64/aarch64) — a mutable object store operated by the same fork publisher, not by the upstream `happy` project — for subsequent execution on the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
